item.title: Seraj Uddin Ahmed’s 41st death anniv today
item.description: Today is the 41st anniversary of death of Seraj Uddin Ahmed, founder chairman of Elite Paint Group of Companies....

item.title: Mob violence rises in April: MSF
item.description: The number of mob violence incidents and deaths in such incidents across the country increased in April compared with March, according to a report published on Thursday by the Manabadhikar Shongskriti Foundation....

item.title: Ukraine seeking details on Russia’s May 9 ceasefire offer
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said Kyiv would ask Washington for more information about a Kremlin offer for a ceasefire on May 9, when Russia holds large-scale WWII commemorations....GeographicReference

item.title: We will win, don’t trust exit polls: Mamata
item.description: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee has alleged that exit polls predicting a BJP win in the assembly election are aimed at hurting the morale of Trinamool Congress workers and leaders, reports NDTV....

item.title: Iran defies US blockade, oil prices soar
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader declared Thursday that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from president Donald Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come....

item.title: Press freedom at lowest level in 25 years: RSF
item.description: Press freedom has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, Reporters Without Borders warned on Thursday....

item.title: Inflation to remain high amid pricey energy: BB
item.description: Bangladesh faces renewed inflationary pressures as global energy prices surge following the ongoing Middle East conflict, according to a report published by the Bangladesh Bank on Thursday......Newspapers

item.title: Govt okays import of 1 lakh tonnes of diesel without tender
item.description: The cabinet committee on government purchase at a meeting on Thursday approved an Energy Division proposal to procure 1 lakh tonnes of diesel without any tender, citing the ongoing war in the Middle East as the reason......Sports news updates

item.title: Islami, Standard, SBAC banks droped to ‘Z’
item.description: The Dhaka Stock Exchange has downgraded three banks to Z-category, commonly known as junk category, after they failed to declare dividends for two consecutive years......

item.title: Biman signs deal with Boeing to buy 14 aircraft
item.description: National flag carrier Biman Bangladesh Airlines Limited on Thursday signed an agreement with the Boeing Company to acquire 14 aircraft, including 10 wide-body 787 Dreamliners and four narrow-body 737 MAX....

item.title: City Bank reports 162pc net profit growth in Q1
item.description: City Bank PLC has reported 162 per cent net profit growth in the first quarter of 2026 that ended on March 31, demonstrating sustained growth across key areas compared to the same period of the previous year, said a press release......

item.title: Agent banking reporting system revised
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has introduced a revised reporting framework for agent banking to improve the quality and detail of monthly data submitted by scheduled banks......Geographic Reference

item.title: Foreign loan receipt down, repayment up
item.description: The commitment and disbursement of loans from foreign sources dropped while the repayment increased in the first three quarters of the outgoing financial year of 2025-26....

item.title: Stocks end week lower
item.description: The equity indices of the Dhaka Stock Exchange closed lower in the final trading session of the week, as investors remained cautious amid ongoing corporate earnings and dividend declarations alongside global uncertainties, particularly developments surrounding Middle East ceasefire negotiations......

item.title: Gold price revised twice in a day
item.description: Gold prices in Bangladesh experienced fluctuations throughout Thursday, reflecting increased volatility in the domestic market......

item.title: First session of 13th JS prorogued
item.description: The first session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad was prorogued on Thursday, with prime minister Tarique Rahman urging lawmakers from both the treasury and opposition benches to embrace a spirit of unity....Bangladesh business insights

item.title: Oil hits 4-year peak
item.description: Oil prices struck a four-year high on Thursday on worries over a resumption of hostilities in the Middle East, while US and European stock markets mostly rose as investors turned to central bank decisions and corporate earnings......

item.title: BB lifts provisioning on funds stuck at 5 crisis-hit banks
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has withdrawn the requirement for banks and financial institutions to maintain provisions against funds stuck in five merged Shariah-based banks....

item.title: Powell’s decision to stay on at Fed ignites new Trump insult
item.description: The extraordinary clash at the Federal Reserve between Jerome Powell and Donald Trump entered new territory Wednesday when the outgoing US central bank chairman said he would remain on as a governor — and the president lashed him with another insult......

item.title: May Day today
item.description: Bangladesh along with the rest of the world is set to observe May Day, also known as International Workers’ Day, today....

item.title: Takaful Islami Insurance holds 150th board meeting
item.description: Takaful Islami Insurance PLC held the 150th meeting of its board of directors on Wednesday, said a press release......

item.title: SCB, MetLife launch prog for employees with disabilities
item.description: Standard Chartered Bangladesh has recently partnered with MetLife Bangladesh to launch a new assistive benefits programme for employees with disabilities, said a press release......

item.title: IBBL launches remittance campaign
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC and Western Union on Wednesday launched ‘Islami Bank-Western Union Remittance Campaign,’ said a press release......

item.title: 27,000 cases pending with labour courts
item.description: Hundreds of workers across the country are facing prolonged delays in securing justice as labour courts and the Labour Appellate Tribunal grapple with a mounting backlog of more than 27,500 cases, raising concerns over access to timely legal redress....

item.title: Trust Bank celebrates Pahela Baishakh
item.description: Trust Bank PLC on Wednesday hosted a closed-door gathering to celebrate Pahela Baishakh, said a press release......

item.title: Esquire Electronics opens new showroom
item.description: Esquire Electronics Limited recently inaugurated a new showroom at Subid Bazar in Sylhet, said a press release......

item.title: No minimum standard of wage for workers yet
item.description: Longstanding structural weaknesses in the Bangladesh labour market — including the absence of a national minimum wage standard and a persistently large informal sector — continue to undermine worker rights and economic security....

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 9 in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes on south Lebanon killed nine people including two children, the health ministry said Thursday, shortly after the president decried what he described as on-going Israeli violations of a nearly two-week ceasefire....

item.title: Modhumoti Bank holds 95th board meeting
item.description: Modhumoti Bank PLC on Wednesday held the 95th meeting of its board of directors, said a press release......

item.title: Flood continues in Netrakona
item.description: The flood situation in the low-lying areas of Netrakona adjacent to haor regions continued on Thursday and may worsen in the next few days, according to the Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre....

item.title: May Day highlights new challenges
item.description: THE world is changing rapidly. Technological advancements — automation, artificial intelligence, and robotics — have ushered in the era of the Fourth Industrial Revolution. This transformation is not limited to industry; it is reshaping labour markets, economies, and social structures. In such a time, International Workers’ Day gains renewed significance as it......

item.title: Empowerment in tide, inequality beneath
item.description: BY THE time I arrived at Porakatla village in Shyamnagar in the afternoon, the heat of the sun had begun to ease. Yet Shyamnagar in Satkhira District remains intensely hot because of the salinity in the water. On top of that, electricity is unavailable for nearly 14 to 17 hours within every 24-hour period. At the three fuel pumps, there were long queues of motorcycles and......

item.title: Quiet crisis of labour
item.description: EVERY year, as the first of May arrives with the red of workers’ flags and the sound of marches, Bangladesh pauses to honour its labour force. It is through the hands of its workers that the country has stitched its place on the global economic map. According to the 2024 Labour Force Survey by the Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics, the labour force stands at about 73.8......Geographic Reference

item.title: May Day and labour law 2026
item.description: THE global recognition of the eight-hour workday did not come easily. It was won through bloody struggles, mass uprisings, and the sacrifice of countless lives. Behind it lies the historic labour movement that emerged 140 years ago in the city of Chicago in the United States, demanding recognition of the eight-hour working day. At the time, workers were forced to......

item.title: Empty May Day observance betrays worker rights
item.description: INTERNATIONAL Workers’ Day, or May Day, is observed today with renewed demands for a national minimum wage standard and legal protections for workers in informal sectors as successive governments have remained non-committal to the issues. The labour reform commission report shows that at least 22 sectors continue to pay workers much less than what......

item.title: Govt must work hard to close vaccination gap
item.description: THE decline in measles vaccination coverage from 88.6 per cent in 2019 to 86 per cent in 2025 for the first dose and from 89 per cent in 2019 to 80.7 per cent in 2023 for the second dose, or full vaccination, is a telltale sign of the failure of health managers. The coverage, which began at 2 per cent in 1985, had increased up to 83.9 per cent in 2019, registering a steady growth in......

item.title: Shaugatul Alam appointed as DMTCL managing director
item.description: Md Shaugatul Alam, director general of NovoTheatre, has been appointed as the managing director of Dhaka Mass Transit Company Limited....Breakingnews alerts

item.title: 2 held over sale of ‘fake’ SSC question papers
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion has arrested two youths for their alleged involvement in a gang selling ‘fake’ Secondary School Certificate examination question papers through social media and online communication platforms in the capital’s Kamrangirchar area....
